paper_title: Scenario Generation for Time Series and Curves: A Comparison of Nonparametric and Semiparametric Bootstrap

stage4_validation.method: synthetic-data smoke test end-to-end (data/make_synthetic.py -> train.py -> scripts/simulate.py -> scripts/evaluate.py -> scripts/plot_figures.py -> inference.py)
checks_performed: py_compile on all generated .py files (pre- and post-fix): passed
train.py --debug (fits StationaryBootstrap, VARBootstrap, NelsonSiegelVARBootstrap on synthetic data): passed
VARBootstrap spectral radius check: 0.9498 (< 1, stationary, as required by Section 3)
scripts/simulate.py for all 3 methods (stationary_bootstrap, var_bootstrap, ns_var_bootstrap): passed
scripts/evaluate.py for var_bootstrap and ns_var_bootstrap: passed, produced plausible metrics (negative-increment %, correlation, arbitrage-violation checks)
scripts/plot_figures.py: passed, produced figure1_ns_sensitivity.png and figure4_curve_snapshot.png
inference.py single-scenario generation for var_bootstrap: passed
Post-cleanup re-compile of full src/ and scripts/ tree: passed

issues_found_and_fixed.issue: The --horizon-years CLI argument was accepted but silently ignored; simulated horizon was always taken directly from config.evaluation.horizon_nodes_daily/monthly regardless of the flag's value.
issues_found_and_fixed.fix: horizon is now computed by scaling the config's per-year node count by args.horizon_years, so the flag has its documented effect.
issues_found_and_fixed.verified: Re-tested with --horizon-years 5 (yielded 1260 daily steps, matching config default) and --horizon-years 1 (yielded 252 daily steps, correctly scaled).

artifacts_cleaned_post_validation: data/raw/*.csv (synthetic test data, git-ignored, not a deliverable)
results/* (test run outputs, git-ignored, not a deliverable)
stage4_validation.note: Validation used a synthetic dataset generated by data/make_synthetic.py because the paper's actual data (MSCI Europe Net TR EUR, 12m Italian BOT yield, HICP ex-tobacco, Italian yield curve) is proprietary and not distributed with the paper. This validates code correctness/executability only, not numeric agreement with Tables 1-11.

stage5_validation.method: Executed both generated notebooks end-to-end via nbclient against a live Jupyter kernel (not just static inspection).

notebooks_produced.purpose: primary reproduction notebook: environment check, install, paper overview, 4 component walkthroughs (StationaryBootstrap, VARBootstrap, NelsonSiegelModel, NelsonSiegelVARBootstrap) with toy-input demos, mini end-to-end fit+simulate+evaluate on synthetic data, paper results comparison, next steps

exploratory notebook: Nelson-Siegel factor sensitivity (Fig. 1 reproduction), interactive ipywidgets curve explorer, simulated trajectory fan chart (Figs. 2-3 reproduction), yield-curve snapshot (Fig. 4 reproduction), cross-methodology no-arbitrage comparison bar chart (Table 11 reproduction)

checks_performed: nbformat.validate() passed on both notebooks (after adding required cell IDs)
Full end-to-end execution via nbclient.NotebookClient against a live python3 kernel: both notebooks executed with zero unhandled errors

reproduce notebook: all 4 component demo cells produced expected output shapes; VAR spectral radius computed as stationary (<1) on toy data; mini end-to-end block (synthetic data -> fit all 3 methods -> simulate -> Section 6/Appendix B metrics) completed successfully
explore notebook: all 4 planned visualizations rendered as image outputs (4/4 confirmed via output inspection); zero errors across all 15 cells
Confirmed both saved .ipynb files have execution_count=null and outputs=[] for every code cell (no pre-run output committed), per Stage 5 Rule 5
Confirmed no hardcoded absolute paths: both notebooks derive project_root via os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), '..')) relative to the notebook's own location

known_environment_specific_cell_behavior.behavior: `pip install -e ..` fails in this sandbox with 'externally-managed-environment' (a pip/OS-level restriction of the validation environment, not a notebook defect). The cell does not raise (no check=True) and prints the error via captured stdout/stderr; the very next cell adds src/ to sys.path directly as a designed CPU/offline-safe fallback, and every subsequent cell ran successfully despite the failed install.

artifacts_cleaned_post_validation: data/raw/*.csv (synthetic test data generated during notebook execution, git-ignored)
/tmp/executed_reproduce.ipynb, /tmp/executed_explore.ipynb (scratch copies used only for output inspection, not part of the deliverable)

stage6_validation.reason: No user experimental results were submitted in this conversation, and the paper's underlying market data (MSCI Europe Net TR EUR, 12m Italian BOT yield, HICP ex-tobacco, Italian yield curve) is proprietary and unavailable. A reproducibility score requires at least one of: user-run results on the real data, or the real data itself. Neither was available, so no score was fabricated -- see comparison/reproducibility_score.json (score: null, requires_manual_review: true).
stage6_validation.hallucination_analysis_basis: architecture_plan.json + generated src/ code vs. sir.json, independent of user results

hallucination_findings.key_finding: model.engine: 'torch' is declared in config.yaml and documented in every model docstring as an optional batched/GPU simulation path, but grep confirms torch is only ever used for RNG seeding in utils/config.py -- no model class (StationaryBootstrap, VARBootstrap, NelsonSiegelVARBootstrap) branches on engine or uses torch tensors for computation. Classified Significant (not Critical) since the default/only working path is numpy and no paper-reported science is affected.

stage6_validation.next_step_to_unblock: Supply real (or licensed-equivalent) data per data/README_data.md, run train.py + evaluate.py, and resubmit results to regenerate this comparison with an actual reproducibility score.
